title: Service Registry Initialization

steps: Call registerService(registration, beanRegistry) → put in serviceRegistrations + registrations list
Create new ServiceRegistration(serviceProvider) — lazy, no instance yet
Put into serviceRegistrations map keyed by serviceProvider.getServiceClass()
NOTE: registrations list NOT updated until service is actually initialized
Cast-check initializedServices map for serviceClass → if found, return immediately (fast path)
Re-check initializedServices inside sync → if found, return
Put initialized service into initializedServices map
→ if returned service == null → return null (aborts init)
→ registerService(registration, service): registration.setService(service), add to registrations list + serviceRegistrations map
→ doInjections(service): iterate all public methods via reflection
→ For each method with @InjectService annotation → doInjection(service, method, injectService)
→ Validate method has exactly 1 parameter (else ServiceDependencyException)
→ Resolve requestedServiceType from annotation or parameter type
→ getService(requestedServiceType) — RECURSIVE call (may trigger nested initialization)
→ If null and required=true → throw ServiceDependencyException(missing service)
Create ListIterator at END of registrations list
Iterate in REVERSE order (LIFO — last registered, first stopped)
→ IOException caught and logged (non-fatal, continues iteration)

decision_points: getService: initializedServices hit? → fast return (no sync)
getService: registration found? → if not → UnknownServiceException
getService: service already set? → skip initializeService
initializeService: created service null? → return null, skip multi-phase init
doInjection: @InjectService.service() set? → use annotation type, else use param type
doInjection: requested service null AND required=true? → ServiceDependencyException
injectDependencies: service instanceof ServiceRegistryAware? → inject registry ref
configureService: service instanceof Configurable? → call configure()
startService: service instanceof Startable? → call start()
stopService: service instanceof Closeable? → call close()
exception_paths: ServiceDependencyException: @InjectService method has wrong param count
key_relationships: DefaultServiceRegistry composes ConcurrentMap<Class, ServiceRegistration> serviceRegistrations (lazy providers)
DefaultServiceRegistry composes ConcurrentMap<Class, Service> initializedServices (live instances)
DefaultServiceRegistry composes List<ServiceRegistration> registrations (ordered, for LIFO stop)
ServiceRegistration<T> holds ServiceProvider<T> (nullable) + volatile T service
ServiceRegistration variant 2: lazy-init (serviceProvider set, service initially null)
@InjectService annotation on setter methods drives reflection-based DI
concurrency_notes: Double-checked locking in getService(); registerService() syncs on registrations list; close() syncs on registrations list; initializedServices is ConcurrentHashMap.
important_notes: BeanRegistry is always pre-registered at construction time
Service init is lazy — triggered on first getService() call
Multi-phase init order: inject → configure → start (always this order)
Stop order is strictly LIFO (reverse of registration order)
stopService IOException is caught and logged but does NOT abort shutdown
doInjections catches all exceptions and logs them (non-fatal for injection loop)
Recursive getService() calls during injection may trigger nested initialization
